7 hours 15 minutes of sleep; Sunday night wind-down into Monday morning, resetting energy for the week.

diary: Sunday was exactly what I needed. Woke up early with the dog—she was restless, so we hit Maple Leaf Park before the heat set in. The morning light through the trees felt restorative after a week of back-to-back calls. Grabbed coffee at home, scrolled through community messages (a few developer questions about the new SDK), nothing urgent. The farmers market in Ballard was packed but energizing. I talked with this vendor about heirloom tomatoes for maybe twenty minutes—something about slowing down and actually listening to people feels rare these days. Came home, cooked lunch with what we'd picked up. Family time on the porch this evening was simple and grounding. Even the evening felt unhurried. By the time I settled in with a book, I realized I hadn't checked Slack once after 3 PM. That's a win.
thought_process: The weather and Sunday quiet created natural permission to step back. I deliberately chose the farmers market over a quick grocery run because I knew I needed human interaction that wasn't transactional—talking with vendors about their crops felt like the opposite of async communication. Work stayed light; I checked messages early but didn't let them pull me into problem-solving mode. The dog walks bookended the day nicely, gave me rhythm without structure. I think I've been running too hard on fumes, so today I was almost protective of my time, not letting 'productivity' creep in. The porch dinner with family crystallized it—I want more days like this, even if I can only carve them out on weekends.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
